body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:error-export

Route: driver-gap (extend the driver's transcript export, then implement).

## Scenario

turn-aborted-by-error: the model call fails mid-turn (provider error / 5xx / timeout / context overflow / unloadable model); no normal turn-end signal is emitted. Primitive exercised: sweep/terminal-based stall detection â€” the daemon must resolve the session to `ready` after the failure. User-observable signal (scenario-meanings): state reaches `ready` after the failure and does NOT stick in `working`.

## agent_supports: yes

opencode 1.14.50 surfaces a turn that aborts by error. It does NOT write a `step-finish` part with `reason="error"`; instead it records the failure on the PARENT MESSAGE row's `message.data.error` field. A prior live store audit (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) found 39 such rows â€” APIError ('Unauthorized' / 'Model not found' / 'Failed to load model â€¦'), UnknownError ('Unexpected server error', context-overflow 'n_keep > context length'), and MessageAbortedError ('Aborted') â€” against 0 step-finish reason=error parts. The feature (a turn can abort by provider/runtime error) demonstrably exists; the `requires: headless_mode` gate is satisfied (capabilities.json `headless_mode: true`).

## daemon_capability: full (CHANGED from the prior `incapable` narrative)

The prior assessment's body declared `incapable` because the opencode watcher's `scanParts` was part-driven and never inspected `message.data.error` â€” so an error turn produced a user `text` part (â†’ working), an assistant message with `error` set and NO `step-finish` part, hence no `turn_done`, and the session stuck in `working` until process exit.

That is NO LONGER TRUE in the current daemon. The watcher now consumes the message-level error as a terminal signal:

- `isErrorMessage(msgData)` (watcher.go:450-465) returns true for a non-empty `error` string or `{name,message,â€¦}` object (fail-open on null/empty/parse error).
- `scanParts` SELECTs `m.data as message_data` (watcher.go:483) and flips `hasTerminal = true` when EITHER `isTerminalPart(partData)` OR `isErrorMessage(msgData)` (watcher.go:528-530), setting `EventActivity.Terminal = hasTerminal` (watcher.go:550).
- `session_detector_activity.go:221-228` short-circuits the debounce on `ev.Terminal`, records `KindDebounceTerminal`, and runs `processActivity` â†’ the turn resolves workingâ†’ready.
- Unit-tested: `TestScanSessions_ErrorMessageTerminal` (watcher_test.go:632) inserts a message with `message.data.error` and a non-terminal text part, and asserts the emitted EventActivity carries `Terminal=true`. `TestIsErrorMessage` covers the string/object/null/false/zero/array shapes. Both pass against current code (`go test ./core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/`).

So, given a recording that carries the message-level error, the daemon AS IT EXISTS TODAY emits the terminal transition the spec requires. daemon_capability = full.

## driver_capability: gap:error-export (the blocker)

The recording cannot reproduce the settle because the interactive driver's transcript export omits the one field the daemon keys on. Both export queries in scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh â€” the headless path (lines 411-427) and the live-TUI path (lines 250-266) â€” project ONLY `part` rows:

    SELECT json_set(p.data, '$._role', json_extract(m.data,'$.role'),
                            '$._cwd',  s.directory,
                            '$._ts',   p.time_updated,
                            '$._model',json_extract(m.data,'$.model.modelID'))
    FROM part p JOIN message m ON p.message_id = m.id ...

They augment each `p.data` with the message ROLE and MODEL, but never carry `message.data.error`. The replay tool (core/cmd/replay/main.go:74) drives the fixture through `opencode.Parser.ParseLine`, which sees only part data â€” and `isErrorMessage` is a watcher-only function reading the raw `m.data` column, with no equivalent in the parser. Hence a recorded transcript for an error-aborted turn contains no terminal signal, and replay would leave the session in `working` even though a LIVE daemon settles it correctly.

This is a tooling gap, not an observability limit: the daemon is capable; the recording harness lacks a step/export primitive to carry the message-level error into the fixture. Named primitive: `error-export`. Tracked as #493.

The new live-TUI path (slash / reset_session under tmux) added since the prior assess does NOT change this: it uses the SAME parts-only export query. The driver's input mechanism is irrelevant to the gap.

## Recordability

Not recordable today (gap:error-export). It becomes recordable once the driver exports a carrier for `message.data.error` into the JSONL fixture â€” e.g. synthesize a terminal part (or a message-error row the replay path can consume) when the parent message carries a non-empty error â€” so the replay parser/watcher path can reproduce the workingâ†’ready settle. A context-overflow prompt or an unloadable/oversized-model prompt is a deterministic elicitation that lands on `message.data.error` (verified previously: a bogus model id is rejected at config resolution BEFORE a session row exists, so use a context-overflow or unloadable-model trigger instead).

## Unblocks when

The `extend-driver opencode error-export` work adds the message-error carrier to the transcript export (both headless and live paths) so the fixture reproduces the daemon's now-working settle. No daemon change is required.
caveats: Driver export gap (#493): the transcript export is parts-only (drive-opencode-interactive.sh:411-427 and :250-266); it carries message role+model but not message.data.error, the field the daemon keys on. Until the export adds a message-error carrier, a replay fixture cannot reproduce the workingâ†’ready settle even though the live daemon does it.

Zero-part errors are the remaining half of #493: the watcher's terminal-detection is part-driven (the partâ†”message JOIN must have a part row to carry msgData), so a turn that errors before emitting ANY part is not surfaced (watcher.go:521-527). The common error shapes (provider error / context overflow / unloadable model) all emit at least a user text part first, so this is a narrow caveat, not the primary blocker.
Probed on opencode 1.14.50 with the default local model (LM Studio, gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored, loaded_context_length 70419).

sources.note: isErrorMessage (450-465) reads message.data.error as a terminal signal; scanParts SELECTs m.data as message_data (483) and sets hasTerminal via isTerminalPart||isErrorMessage (528-530), emitting EventActivity.Terminal (550). This is the code that makes daemon_capability=full.

TestScanSessions_ErrorMessageTerminal (632) asserts an errored turn (message.data.error, no step-finish part) yields EventActivity.Terminal=true; TestIsErrorMessage (601-625) covers the error JSON shapes. Both pass against current code.
Line 221-228: ev.Terminal short-circuits the debounce, records KindDebounceTerminal, and runs processActivity â€” the workingâ†’ready settle the spec requires.

Transcript export (headless 411-427, live-TUI 250-266) is parts-only: json_set(p.data, _role/_cwd/_ts/_model). It never projects message.data.error â€” the gap:error-export blocker. core/cmd/replay/main.go:74 drives the fixture through opencode.Parser.ParseLine, which only sees part data.

Prior live store audit: step-finish reasons = stop(102), tool-calls(165), error(0). message.data.error rows = 39 (APIError/UnknownError/MessageAbortedError incl. context-overflow n_keep>context length). Errors persist at the message level, never as a step-finish part â€” so the daemon's message-level path (not the step-finish path) is what observes this scenario.
